name: The Story of the Moving Image
description:
From the first projections and optical illusions to the birth of film and beyond, moving images have the power to spark imagination, share stories and shape history. Discover how inventors, innovators and artists at the turn of the 20th century wielded light, split time and captured motion, heralding a technological revolution that continues today.

Featuring ancient shadow puppets, Victorian-era magic lanterns, original cameras, iconic costumes, movie sets, sketches, clips and contemporary art,
venue: ACMI (Australian Centre for the Moving Image)

name: Making Memoir of a Snail
description:
To coincide with the international premiere of Academy Award-winning writer / director Adam Elliot's Memoir of a Snail during the Melbourne International Film Festival (MIFF), ACMI is displaying a series of handmade sets, props and characters from the film.

See the creative and technical processes of stop motion claymation, presented through a selection of the thousands of items that were meticulously handmade by a skilled team of artists. Shot frame by frame, the film contains no CGI; every pr

name: Aboriginal Heritage Walk
description:
Wominjeka! Journey into Royal Botanic Gardens Melbourne, a significant cultural site for the local Kulin Nation, with a First Peoples guide. 

Gain insight into the rich history and thriving culture of the First Peoples of Australia through a journey identifying significant native plants within the Gardens. Learn about Aboriginal plant uses, customs and ongoing connection to Country.

Please note this tour does not run on Christmas Day or New Year's Day.
venue: Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria - Melbourne Gardens

name: Cabaret Ridiculous
description:
Start your evening with a dose of the weird, the wacky, and the wonderful at one of Melbourne’s most charming theatres. Cabaret Ridiculous offers an hour of variety entertainment that’s as unpredictable as it is unforgettable.

Each show brings a fresh lineup of performances, ranging from dazzling costumes and captivating burlesque to hilarious comedy. Some acts will leave you in awe, while others might leave you scratching your head – but that’s all part of the fun.

This Vegas-style cabaret th

name: The Sunday Market at Southgate
description:
After decades positioned around the Arts Centre, the Sunday Market is now at Southgate.

Complemented by some of Melbourne’s long standing and much-loved cafes and restaurants.

Come and explore, meet the makers and artists face-to-face, and learn about their products and what inspires their work. All Melbourne Made; take home a piece of Melbourne.

Stallholders are positioned in marquees along the Yarra River as well as on the ground level of Southgate.

name: Run the Tan: Run Club
description:
Walk, jog, or run around The Tan, with like-minded fitness enthusiasts.

No pressure, no judgment, no expectations. This event caters to all levels of fitness and abilities and is supported by qualified running coaches. It's a perfect way to start the weekend, improve your fitness and technique, and meet new people on the track or over coffee at the finish line.

name: Mystery Sidedoor City: Wine and Asian Fusion Bites
description:
A Mystery Sidedoor is a unique way to discover a new venue through an off-menu tasting experience, complete with perfectly paired drinks.

Guided by a digital experience on your smartphone, you’ll arrive at a hidden gem in Melbourne’s city centre to enjoy a trio of Asian fusion bites and a curated wine tasting flight.

Along the way, enjoy interactive games and conversation starters to keep things fun and engaging. Plus, you’ll unlock an exclusive beverage discount at a nearby venue if you want

name: Yarra Sunrise Running Tour
description:
Start your day with the beauty of Melbourne on this early morning Yarra Sunrise Running Tour. This 8km (5-mile) guided run begins at Federation Square. As Melbourne wakes, be guided through parklands and past iconic landmarks, sharing stories of the city’s history, sporting culture, and hidden gems.

Run along Birrarung Marr to the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG), cross the Yarra River, and tackle Anderson Street hill on the famous Tan Track – a favourite for local runners. Pause at the Shrine of

name: State Library Victoria Unlocked
description:
Join an exclusive guided tour and experience State Library Victoria’s grand Dome and hidden spaces before the doors open to the public. Advance bookings essential. 

Ever heard of the ‘Chief Shusher’? Wonder what’s buried beneath the Library’s floors? Or gazed up at the Library Dome before the crowds?  

On this early access tour, you’ll explore the Library’s magnificent heritage spaces and catacombs hidden beneath the library floors, discover the treasure trove of original card catalogues, and

name: Melbourne Magic Show
description:
Experience an evening of mind-blowing magic and illusion performed inches from your eyes. Master magician Luke Hocking showcases his live show, Impossible Occurrences.

The lights are dim and you take your seat in an elegant back room of a five-star Melbourne hotel. The stage is set, the mood is relaxed and the guests are ready to witness the impossible. Your host is Luke Hocking,  a local modern-day magician who fuses illusion and mind reading into a theatrical magic experience like no other. A

name: Lunchtime Concerts
description:
Take a break and enjoy a lunchtime concert on Wednesdays.

These half-hour lunchtime recitals provide fine music and space for contemplation in the midst of a busy week, encouraging city workers and others to come in and enjoy the beautiful surroundings of the Cathedral during the middle of the day.

Upcoming concerts:
1pm Wed 4 March: Josh Hooke (piano) 
1pm Wed 11 March: En blanc et noir – Katrina Choi (piano)  
1pm Wed 18 March: Ethan Zhang (piano) 
1pm Wed 25 March: Qualia Ensemble (viola an

name: Game Worlds
description:
Spanning over 50 years of play, Game Worlds features more than 30 videogames from the 1970s to today, with 44 playable moments alongside original builds, rare design materials and hands-on prototypes you won’t find anywhere else.

Explore major titles like Minecraft, Final Fantasy XIV Online, The Elder Scrolls Online, SimCity and Stardew Valley, alongside World of Warcraft, The Sims, Neopets and the highly anticipated Hollow Knight: Silksong.

Discover four new microgames made exclusively for AC
venue: ACMI (Australian Centre for the Moving Image)

name: Order Up: A City Fed by Many Cultures
description:
Hungry for more than just a meal? Step into Order Up: A City Fed by Many Cultures, the Immigration Museum’s newest immersive installation. 

Suspended in the air are thousands of handwritten, food-stained dockets. Each one, a fleeting moment from the kitchens of 33 iconic Melbourne restaurants. These aren’t just orders, they’re memories, movement, and the heartbeat of a city built on multicultural dining and shared stories.

Order Up: A City Fed by Many Cultures is a collaboration between Melbou

name: Swinging 60s
description:
The 1960s is remembered as a turbulent decade. In contrast to the ‘conservative’ 1950s, the sixties are associated with changing ideas, youthful rebellion and experimentation. New music, new fashions and new attitudes to authority defined ‘the generation gap’.

In this exhibition we look at what it was like to live in Victoria during the 1960s. How different were the 1960s from the decades before or after? Was it really all about sex, drugs and rock and roll? Did the sixties really swing in Vict

name: Sunday Night Stand Up Comedy Show
description:
Established in 2003, Club Voltaire showcases its Sunday night stand-up comedy show with some of Melbourne's best stand-up comedians from television, the Melbourne International Comedy Festival, Edinburgh Fringe Festival, Australia Raw Comedy Finals, interstate acts, local legends, and future stars of comedy.

There's a new lineup each week.

name: Serene Terrarium: Workshop for Beginners
description:
Discover the enchanting world of terrariums in this engaging terrarium class. You’ll learn how to craft your very own miniature garden, choosing between an open-top or closed-top glass vessel.

Follow step-by-step instructions to build your terrarium and gain insights on how to maintain and care for your tiny world. You’ll be provided with all the necessary materials to bring your terrarium to life. As you nurture your creation, you’ll feel a sense of satisfaction and joy in creating something b

name: Mosaic Making Workshop for Beginners
description:
In this mosaic workshop, you will learn the art of creating beautiful mosaics from start to finish.

Choose from a variety of colourful glass and learn how to design, stick and grout your own unique piece. No artistic experience is necessary, as you will be guided by an expert through the entire process.

Enjoy a fun and hands-on experience working with a range of materials, adhesives, glass tiles and grouts used for indoor and outdoor mosaics.

Take home your completed mosaic creation, which wi

name: Colourful Mosaic Coaster Workshop
description:
Learn the art of creating stunning mosaics in this hands-on workshop.

You'll be guided through designing, sticking and grouting your own unique piece using a variety of colourful glass. No artistic experience is required.

Create your own mosaic masterpiece to take home, a beautiful reminder of your time spent exploring this magical art form.

name: The Explorer Bus Melbourne
description:
Discover the beauty of this inner-city oasis and enjoy live commentary in the comfort of an open-air minibus on a 30-minute tour. Admire rolling lawns and the stunning living collections at Royal Botanic Gardens Melbourne.

Runs daily except Christmas Day and New Year’s Day. Children ride for half-price on the School Explorer Bus during the school holidays.
venue: Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria - Melbourne Gardens

name: Free Guided Walk
description:
Enjoy a free guided walk through Royal Botanic Gardens Melbourne, one of the city’s most beautiful attractions. Led by a knowledgeable and passionate guide, this experience immerses you in stunning landscapes, rare and unusual plants, and fascinating stories. It’s the perfect introduction for visitors wanting to connect with nature while discovering this world-renowned botanic garden.

Please note that there will be no Free Guided Walk tours on Friday 3 April (Good Friday) and Sunday 10 May (Mot
venue: Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria - Melbourne Gardens

name: Mosaic Art Classes: Turkish Lamp Workshop
description:
Think you’re not creative? Let us change your mind.

Join us for an unforgettable craft evening of creativity and relaxation at our Turkish lamp workshop. Whether you’re an emerging artist or simply curious to try something new, this experience is designed for everyone.

No experience needed — just come ready to have fun! We’ll guide you step-by-step as you design your own mosaic lamp. You’ll be surprised by what you can create with a little guidance.

All materials are provided — lamp base, mos

name: Moonlight Cinema
description: Enjoy movies under the stars with your favourite cult classics, box office blockbusters and family flicks.
venue: Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria - Melbourne Gardens

name: Comedy at the Coopers Inn
description:
Start the weekend with a laugh – Comedy at the Coopers Inn showcases the best local, interstate and international stand-up comedians. 

With different and diverse line-ups every Friday and Saturday there's something for everyone, including big names from TV and the most exciting new talent in the country. In a dedicated comedy club and bar. 

Past acts have included Tom Gleeson, Celia Pacquola, Luke McGregor, Anne Edmonds, Dilruk Jayasinha, Rhys Nicholson, Geraldine Hickey, Cal Wilson, Nick Cody

name: NGV Friday Nights
description:
Each Friday, the NGV is open late. 

Experience the NGV as it comes alive after dark – think fashion, art, icons and beats. Your Friday Nights ticket includes unlimited entry to Westwood | Kawakubo, an exhibition of works by Vivienne Westwood and Rei Kawakubo of Comme des Garçons. 

From 6–10pm, enjoy live DJ sets, plus food and drinks available to purchase on the night.

name: Hany Armanious: Stone Soup
description:
In Stone Soup nothing is as it seems. Leading Australian artist Hany Armanious transforms everyday objects, from crackers and candles to paint trays and lumps of Blu-Tack, into near-perfect doubles that make the familiar feel newly alive.
 
His practice unsettles what we think we know, asking us to pause, look closer and rediscover the joy of seeing things as if for the very first time. 

Featuring more than 80 works spanning 15 years of practice, including many never before seen in Australia, t

name: Racquet Club
description:
Crown Racquet Club has landed on the Yarra. Set directly on Melbourne’s iconic Crown Riverwalk, this is Racquet Club’s most ambitious pop-up yet, a high-energy fusion of world-class padel, waterfront atmosphere and unmistakable Crown polish. With two state-of-the-art panoramic padel courts framed by the river, a DJ booth setting the tempo and hot coffee served from 6am by our partner Vittoria, Crown Racquet Club is built to be watched, played and experienced.

Off court, the experience goes furt

name: Critical Hits Music Bingo
description:
Critical Hits Music Bingo is a free video game and pop-culture themed music bingo night filled with live performances, singalongs, dancing and exciting games such as the Nerf Gun Challenge.

Your host is Jacob brings together acoustic covers, theatrical performances, a love of all things video games, epic fantasy, sci-fi and more to create an incredible experience for you and your friends.

name: The Harmonix Experience
description:
Every second week at Bird’s Basement, The Harmonix Experience takes the stage – and no prisoners. This genre-bending outfit blends jazz, acid jazz, funk and a splash of rock into a sound that’s as unpredictable as it is irresistible.

Led by Albare on guitar, with Jake Mason on Hammond, Phil Rex on bass, Andrew Swann on drums, Paul Williamson on alto sax and Salva Persico on percussion, the band kicks off each set with a simple sketch – then lets instinct and groove take over.

Expect deep groov

name: Learn to Cook Thai: Pad Thai and Papaya Salad
description:
Join the team at BangPop for an incredible hands-on cooking experience featuring two of Thailand’s most beloved dishes: pad Thai and green papaya salad.

At their popular masterclass series, BangPop's expert chef will guide you through the process of creating these two classic Thai dishes. You’ll learn the secrets to perfecting the balance of sweet, sour, salty, and spicy flavours that are essential to pad Thai and green papaya salad. All while enjoying the breathtaking views of the Yarra River

name: Footsteps Through Time: Melbourne's History Walk
description:
Step into the past with this small-group Melbourne history walking tour, limited to 10 guests for a more personal and engaging experience. Over two hours, you’ll uncover surprising stories and hidden histories — from the city’s riverside beginnings to the landmarks, laneways and institutions that define it today.

Starting near the Yarra River, the walk explores early European settlement and Melbourne’s rapid transformation. You’ll visit sites of ambition, protest, injustice and reform — often o

name: Sports Precinct Walking Tour
description:
Melbourne truly is the sporting capital of Australia. It is the only city to have six international standard sporting facilities within walking distance of the CBD, and hosts some of the world's best international events, including the Australian Open Tennis Championship and the Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix.  And that's before you even touch on our obsession with Australian Rules Football.

Take a walking tour through the sports precinct with a sports-mad tour host who will show you the sport

name: 7 Paintings Dining Experience
description:
Immerse yourself in a world where each dinner course tells a new story. Colours, shapes, and flavours blend into a journey through seven fascinating masterpieces – staged live on your table.

Over two-and-a-half hours, the famous Mona Lisa takes diners on a visual journey of storytelling, art, lighting, sound, and specially crafted dishes. Each dining session is limited to just 40 seats to ensure an intimate experience.
 
The 7-course menu takes inspiration from the most famous and influential a

name: Ports and Docklands Cruise
description:
Take the Ports and Docklands (Cruise A) sightseeing cruise to view the contrasting scenery that has made Melbourne one of the most liveable cities in the world.

Cruise past Melbourne's city centre and see where the early settlers landed. Now one of the busiest trading ports in the southern hemisphere, Victoria Harbour provides a snapshot of Melbourne's rich maritime heritage in stark contrast to the new and modern Docklands area.

Cruises depart from the ticket kiosk on Southbank Promenade at B

name: Ballet of Lights: Sleeping Beauty in a Sparkling Show
description:
Ballet of Lights: Sleeping Beauty reimagines the classic tale with glow-in-the-dark costumes and luminous staging that blend tradition with innovation. 

Follow Aurora from a fateful curse to a joyous awakening, as dancers paint the stage with colour, silhouette and light. Each twirl and leap leaves shimmering trails, turning familiar scenes into an otherworldly vision. Precision footwork, elegant pas de deux and playful ensemble moments highlight the score and storytelling, while illuminated de

name: Piccolo Teatro: Magic Theatre
description:
Piccolo Teatro Melbourne specialises in astonishing magic shows and immersive live entertainment.

Discreetly nestled in one of Melbourne's iconic city laneways, this boutique magic theatre, seating a cosy 32, blends French Gothic design with the old-world historic charm that Melbourne's laneways are famous for.

Accessible only by a secluded door and an ascending wooden staircase, the space offers a uniquely bespoke experience, with sophisticated illusion, mind reading and magic shows magic per

name: Perfect Pairings: Guided Cheese and Wine Matching
description:
Join sommelier Antony or chef Sigrid in the 170-year-old secret cellars of the former Victoria Brewery for an intimate afternoon of global cheese and wine matching, guided tastings and thoughtful pairing insights, leaving your taste buds yearning for more.

Learn how seven artisan cheeses pair with seven fine back-vintage wines in this guided tasting.

Refer to the ticket link for further details.

name: Travel Sketching with Watercolour Pencil
description:
Note: this event is sold out.

Take an inspirational walk around East Melbourne, sketching with watercolour pencils to record your experience and learning tips and techniques along the way.

name: Cocktail Making Class
description:
Mix, shake and sip your way through the ultimate cocktail class inside a hidden Melbourne bar. Whether you're celebrating something special or just looking for a great afternoon out, this is hands-on, boozy fun from the moment you walk in.

Held in a stylish, tucked-away cocktail bar known for its creative drinks and welcoming vibe, Behind the Bar is a shared experience that brings cocktail lovers together for an interactive and unforgettable event. Kick things off with a welcome drink and some

name: Auslan Storytime
description:
Come along every 1st and 3rd Monday of each month (excluding public holidays and Victorian school holidays) for Auslan Storytime by Deaf storytellers from Expression Australia.

Auslan Storytime brings stories to life through visual language, expression and movement – delivered entirely in Australian Sign Language (Auslan).

This is an inclusive early years program designed to be engaging and accessible for Deaf, hard-of-hearing and hearing children alike. Sessions are delivered directly in Ausl

name: Stompers
description:
Encourage your child to have a lifelong love of books at Stompers at Kathleen Syme Library, every Monday. Enjoy fun stories, songs, rhymes and activities with your child.

Age: 18 months to 3 years.

Programs run during school terms and stop during school holidays.

Term 1: Monday 2 February to Thursday 2 April
Term 2: Monday 20 April to Friday 26 June
Term 3: Monday 13 July to Friday 18 September
Term 4: Monday 5 October to Friday 11 December
venue: Kathleen Syme Library and Community Centre

name: Drag Bingo
description:
Bingo just got a lipstick-stained makeover.

Head to Heartbreaker for Drag Bingo hosted by one of Melbourne’s finest queens and fuelled by cheap drinks, Connie’s pizza slices, and rock n’ roll.

Balls drop at 7pm and it's free to play. Every damn Tuesday.

name: Rainbow Art Class
description:
Create great memories for your kids by choosing stimulating and exciting things for them to do.

These art classes are all about having fun with self-expression, creativity and imagination. Experienced art teacher Shyamasree Bose guides children in a colourful exploration of acrylic painting.

For ages 5–16 years and no experience is required.

name: English Reading Circle
description:
Read along with others and practice pronunciation. Improve your English comprehension and vocabulary. Meet new people in a safe, welcoming environment.

The program welcomes both native and EAL (English as an additional language) speakers.

name: Songbirds
description:
Songs, rhymes and stories will engage your budding bookworm’s mind and introduce them to the fun and rhythm of language.

Age: newborn to 18 months and their carers

Programs run during school terms and stop during school holidays.

Term 1: Monday 2 February to Thursday 2 April
Term 2: Monday 20 April to Friday 26 June
Term 3: Monday 13 July to Friday 18 September
Term 4: Monday 5 October to Friday 11 December

name: High Tea at Great Ocean Road Chocolaterie and Ice Creamery
description:
Enjoy a decadent high tea served at private tables in the Chocolaterie's Pod Café. 

Enjoy a refreshing drink on arrival, followed by decadent petite delights, scones, ribbon sandwiches and pastries created by their team of talented chefs and French chocolatiers plus endless organic local teas, barista coffee and real hot chocolates. 

The Chocolaterie welcomes guests for Weekday High Teas Monday to Friday during school terms (these sessions are for adults only). They also offer Seniors High Tea

name: Louise Donovan: Common Ground
description:
Melbourne printmaker Louise Donovan presents Common Ground, a captivating exhibition capturing everyday moments from Melbourne, Creswick and Alice Springs. Working in her signature drypoint on copper, Donovan translates docks, Port Phillip Bay, intimate interiors, architectural forms and her unmistakable trees into quietly powerful works rich with immediacy and texture.

Trained at the Victorian College of the Arts, Donovan’s work is held in the National Gallery of Australia and continues to fea

name: Lotus Flower Season
description:
The Blue Lotus Water Garden is one of Australia's most spectacular seasonal gardens. Each year the gardens holds two stunning floral events starting with the famous Lotus Flower Season, followed by the beautiful Autumn Flower Season. 

During the Lotus Season, visitors will experience the world's most extensive collection of lotus flower varieties which produce millions of huge flowers spread over 14 acres of water gardens. Highlights include Lotus Lake and Lotus Wetlands with thousands of bloom
address: Warburton Highway, Yarra Junction VIC 3797, Australia

name: Bedtime Storytime
description:
Head to narrm ngarrgu Library for a cosy bedtime story, held on the third Tuesday of every month. Hop into your pyjamas, and bring your favourite teddy.

Ages: 2 to 7 years.
venue: narrm ngarrgu Library and Family Services

name: Improvised Comedy: F#*k This, I Quit
description:
Ever had a terrible job? Have a story about it? Bring your story down to The Improv Conspiracy Theatre where we’ll spin your workplace woe into a hilarious show.

F#*k This, I Quit is an amalgamation of your worst work stories and improvised comedy. Check out some of the company’s most skilled performers create comedy gold from your horrific tales of bad bosses, callous customers and coldhearted colleagues.

name: Discover Local Social Enterprises: Tour
description:
The Purpose Precinct is a first-of-its-kind marketplace that brings Victorian social enterprises together under multiple roofs. Located in the heart of Melbourne's iconic Queen Victoria Market, the Purpose Precinct showcases social enterprises that have goodness in their DNA – that only exist to do good for people and planet. The Purpose Precinct is also a space to nurture ideas, foster innovation, host events and give budding social entrepreneurs a space to kickstart their business. 

Join this

name: Younghusband Market
description:
Step into a world of creativity at the brand-new Younghusband Market.

Bringing the signature Rose Street Market vibe to the historic Younghusband, a new village-like precinct designed for Melbourne’s movers and shakers, each month is an incredible celebration of creativity and community as the old woolshed transforms into a buzzing hub of art, design and good times.

Expect a huge line-up of local makers, creators and innovators showcasing their unique offerings. With great music, curated stall

name: Bakery Hill Whisky Distillery Tour and Tasting
description:
Bakery Hill is a working distillery and not always open to the public.

This 90-minute tour includes a detailed guided tour of the distillery, conducted by a distiller, where you will learn how Bakery Hill produces its malts. Touch, smell and taste all the exciting elements of single malt whisky production, munch on malted barley, smell the peat, and try spirit straight from a barrel.

The tour includes a tasting of the core Bakery Hill Single Malt range (six samples) and the opportunity to purc

name: Matter of Methods l Methods of Matter
description:
This exhibition reveals the unseen, vulnerable side of creative research.

Featuring sketches, journals, discarded models and experimental work from Melbourne School of Design doctoral candidates, the exhibition celebrates the messy, uncertain processes that shape architectural thinking.

Exploring how uncertainty, joy, and even pain can shape architectural thinking, this exhibition invites people into the private, human and precious moments where creation silently forms.

Curated by Dr Guillerm

name: DIY Craft Workshops
description:
Unleash your creativity at these Lightning Planet workshops, where everyone is a designer.

Lightning Planet is a crafted workshop that provides materials and a welcoming space to perfect your handmade creations, ideal for individuals or groups.

Enjoy a fun craft experience and take home your own design work with options ranging from shoes, T-shirts and bracelets to earrings and keychains.
venue: Lightning Planet DIY Crafts Workshop & Party Arts_Chinatown

name: Stories of Blessing: Modern Echoes of Minhwa by Minhwa Group
description:
Minhwa is a traditional form of Korean folk art that flourished during the late Joseon Dynasty.

Traditionally displayed in homes to invite blessings and ward off misfortune, Minhwa is rich with symbolic motifs: peonies for prosperity, lotus flowers and ducks for vitality and harmony, and countless images conveying hopes and aspirations. Its expressive, playful style captures honesty, humor, and heartfelt desires.

This exhibition explores Minhwa’s cultural significance while presenting contempo

name: Behind the Lines: Are We Rolling?
description:
All the twists and turns of another turbulent year in Australian politics are showcased in this exhibition.

Drawing inspiration from the wonderful world of movie making, this year’s exhibition features 45 of Australia’s finest political cartoonists. Using humour, satire and pathos, they have turned in star performances to interpret this year’s drama and intrigue, here at home and overseas.

Behind the Lines this year celebrates its 30th year, making it one of the longest-running annual exhibiti

name: Off the Beaten Lane: An Insider's Walk
description:
Melbourne reveals itself differently when you walk with an artist. David sees layers where others see surfaces – stories imprinted in bluestone, centuries buried beneath your feet, a city that shares its secrets with those who pause to listen.

This intimate walk for no more than six curious souls traces Melbourne from its foundation to the cosmopolitan city it has become.

Encounter street art as visual poetry, uncovering stories about the artists and the meaning behind their works. Hear tales

name: International Women's Day High Tea at Mary Eats Cake
description:
Mary Eats Cake's specialty International Women’s Day High Tea is the perfect way to honour the spirit of International Women's Day. 

Indulge in a delectable array of sweet and savoury treats while you celebrate with the beautiful women in your life. 

Reserve your table today and join the team in celebrating women, their accomplishments, and the bonds that unite all.

name: Sea Shanties and Folk Songs
description:
If you like singing and want to try something new, then come down to the Mission to Seafarers for a free rollicking singalong of shanties, folk songs, work songs and drinking songs, every Thursday.

No shanty experience or singing ability is needed, and newcomers are very welcome.

These spirited songs are easy to learn, so you can join in with the refrains and choruses, and even lead a song yourself. If needed, there’s an ample amount of liquid courage at the bar.

Purchasing drinks and snacks

name: Focus on Wim Wenders
description:
Wim Wenders has had many lives as a filmmaker. One of cinema’s greatest journeymen, his filmography displays an ongoing curiosity and exploration of the cinema form, and international stories.

Emerging out of the New German cinema wave of the 1970s, Wenders quickly carved a name for himself. Traversing the glove, Wenders brings his own brand of humanism to films such as the Cuban music documentary Buena Vista Social Club (1999), the portrait of Brazilian photographer Juliano Ribeiro Salgado in

name: Matinees at ACMI Cinemas
description:
Summer is ending, but your gateway to escapism is right here. Matinee sessions run every Friday, Saturday and Sunday throughout Autumn, with new films beginning every fortnight.

Journey to the suburbs of Sydney with Jane Campion’s 1989 feature debut Sweetie, recently restored to 4K. From there, take a daytrip to Lagos, Nigeria, during the tumultuous 1993 presidential election with My Father's Shadow. Recover from a car accident in Germany with Christian Petzold’s new film Mirrors No. 3. And in

name: Pressed Flower Lamp Making Workshop
description:
Learn how to create a beautifully unique lamp using delicate pressed flowers to brighten up your space, in a workshop suitable for beginners.

Botanical pressed flower art involves using real dried and flattened flowers and leaves to create intricate compositions. This art form is popular for home decor, often presented in floating glass frames or as part of herbarium collections.

Your friendly teacher guides you through the workshop, ensuring a fun and stress-free experience. You will learn ho

name: Abundance: The Pleasure of Plenty
description:
A group exhibition exploring the pleasure found in the cultivation of beauty. Featuring new work by Margaret Ackland, Elizabeth Barnett, Chelsea Gustafsson, Michelle Molinari, Bronwyn Hill, Miranda Joy Summers, Jacob Leary and guest artist Narelle Zeller.

Epicurus believed that the purpose of life was to seek pleasure – not indulgence without thought, but the art of living well. True pleasure, for him, was found in beauty, friendship, nature and the quietude of a life lived in appreciation of i

name: Afternoon Tea at InterContinental Melbourne The Rialto
description:
Set beneath the stunning glass atrium of Alluvial Restaurant, this luxurious afternoon tea experience at InterContinental Melbourne The Rialto intertwines the vibrant flavours of Australia’s native ingredients with the refined charm of their iconic 1890s architecture.

Curated by Executive Chef Charlie Song, the menu celebrates Australia’s rich culinary landscape and includes a glass of Champagne. From Tasmanian smoked salmon on brioche with finger lime to classic buttermilk scones with Yarra Va

description:
Visit the city's musical busking centre at the Bourke Street Mall.

name: Brimbank Writers and Readers Festival
description:
Now in its 21st year, the Brimbank Writers and Readers Festival is a vibrant celebration of reading, storytelling and creativity. It brings together writers, readers and the wider community to explore ideas, share stories and spark a love of literature for all ages.

Over six action-packed days, the festival features interactive workshops, author talks, panel discussions, creative and professional writing sessions, storytelling, and more. 

Highlights this year include opening night with beloved
address: Collins Street, Saint Albans VIC 3021, Australia

name: Open Booth Foundation presents: Open Decks
The Pandas of the Adelaide Zoo
description:
Wang Wang and Fu Ni are the pride of Adelaide Zoo — rare, beloved, and under enormous pressure to conceive Australia's first baby panda. There’s just one catch. They can’t stand each other.

The Pandas of the Adelaide Zoo is a warm, absurd, and new Australian play, written by Ryan Smith, about love on demand, awkward relationships, and captivity. Watched on by Ranger Hayley and Keeper Trev, Wang Wang and Fu Ni are left to figure out whether romance can be forced, or if some things just aren’t me

name: Sheffield Shield 2025-26 - South Australia vs Victoria
address: Lakeside Dr, St Kilda VIC 3182, Australia

name: Dinash and Friends
description:

Arrive early to enjoy the family fun atmosphere and activities around the stadium pre-game, before heading inside to experience the buzzing atmosphere inside AAMI Park.

Get behind the Victory and secure your seat.

description:
Join the Gender Justice Project for the launch of the Artivism Circle workshop as part of International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

Often referred to as Harmony Day in Australia, returning to the original name acknowledges that we are far from living in a racism-free society. This workshop invites participants into an open conversation about the experiences of women and gender-diverse people of colour in Australia.

Guided by a facilitator, participants will create artwork

Presented by the Australian National Veterans Arts Museum, this performance evening officially launches the Festival of Veterans Arts 2026 (FOVA26). The event brings together Veteran Performing Artists working across contemporary and classical music, theatre, spoken word, experimental sound and choral performance.

Rug up and dive into a lineup of seasonal street food. The Flavours of Autumn Festival is set to transform C Shed with an array of seasonal flavours. Sip something warm (or sparkling) at the pop-up bar – think Fig Gin, Pomegranate Margaritas and a Sparkling Mandarin Sunrise. On the menu? Autumn favourites taking centre stage, from mushrooms and pears to sweet potatoes and more.

Part of the Autumn Series.

In partnership with Melbourne Food & Wine Festival.

description:
The Veterans Public Arts Trail is believed to be the world’s only guided walking trail dedicated to public art created by veterans. Hosted by the Australian National Veterans Arts Museum, the trail highlights the significant contribution of the Veteran Community to Melbourne’s built environment through sculptures, murals and buildings designed or supported by veterans as artists, architects and arts patrons.

Just over one hour from Melbourne, this immersive twilight experience invites visitors to explore a boutique flower farm as the sun sets, gather their own blooms, and enjoy an evening of food, music, and connection in a serene country setting.

Stroll along grassy pathways lined with dahlias, zinnias, and cosmos glowing in the golden hour light. Breathe in the scent of fresh blooms and listen as gentle acous

What started in San Francisco as a humble 15-person cake swap has grown into an event that sells out tickets by the thousand in London and New York. Cake Picnic has captured the world's attention with a delicious proposition: you bring an amazing cake to the picnic, and then you get to eat everyone else's amazing cakes. That's a winning ratio of one whole cake for every person in attendance.

Part of the Melbourne Food and Wine Festival.

description:
Taxi Kitchen’s hit Sips and Snacks series returns for the Melbourne Food and Wine Festival with a four-hour tasting adventure designed for curious food lovers. Move through four interactive producer-led stations, each featuring hands-on experiences, delicious snacks, surprising ingredients, and perfectly matched drinks.

Across a series of immersive 40-minute sessions, guests will meet passionate makers, learn insider techniques, and explore local flavours in creative new ways. It’s social, fun,

name: The Rialto Run-Up
description:
Inspired by the iconic Empire State Building Run-Up, the Rialto Run-Up was the first stair climbing event in Australia, originally taking place in the late 1980s, which quickly became an annual event. This year, participants will ascend the towering 54 floors of the Rialto Building in a challenge of stamina, endurance and community spirit, finishing on the 55th floor. Each step will help provide life-changing education and support for autistic individuals and their families.

At just seven years of age this insanely talented comedian, actor and singer received the Golden Buzzer from Shane Jacobson on Australia's Got Talent, leading to an invite to the USA to perform on America's Got Talent: The Champions.

Expect an evening of cheeky, clever and unfiltered comedy that explores life, school, parents, girls and video games – all through the eyes of one of the world’s most exciting young comedians.

Part of the Melbourne International Comedy Festival.

Join Claire Lyon, internationally renowned soprano and celebrated star of The Phantom of the Opera, as she brings the magic of Broadway to life.

Claire has transfixed audiences with her soaring voice and captivating stage presence, breathing new energy into the world’s most beloved show tunes.

Brighten up your midweek with an elevated edition of Jazz and Shiraz. Sip a drop (or two) of your favourite premium Italian wines, blend in smooth live jazz, add sumptuous Italian cuisine, and finish with great company – the perfect recipe for an unforgettable night.

description:
Club Club is the late-night comedy destination where no two shows are the same, every Wednesday to Sunday for the duration of the Melbourne International Comedy Festival. Club Club sees some of comedy’s biggest names take part in its most unhinged event.

They've got 20 unique shows, including '100 Comedians for $10', 'Bloketopia' and 'Totally Improvised Human Centipede'! Full roster of shows to be announced soon.

With cheap drinks at Theory Bar, you can sit back and enjoy the mayhem. It's free

description:
Experience an evening of bold flavours, cocktail pairings and local connections with The Up Hour. 
This guided aperitivo adventure brings together mini cocktails and single-bite creations that tell a story of sustainability, craft, and sense of place, with each pairing designed for a refined burst of flavour.

Throughout the evening, meet local beverage partners as they share their small-batch spirits, artisanal mixers and the stories behind each ingredient. These collaborations celebrate though

description:
Join this unforgettable evening with Devamrita Swami, a monk, educator, writer and speaker. Discover the secrets to lasting happiness and fulfillment in the midst of life's everyday challenges with this insightful talk on The Happiness Project: Demystifying Success and Satisfaction. Explore the possibilities of finding joy and contentment while navigating the daily grind.

Accompanied by an evening of soulful live music meditation where you can unwind and connect with your inner self. Gain pract

description: Learn about different types of plastics and what can (and can’t) be recycled. Then get involved – sort and process PLA scraps to be transformed into new objects with our benchtop extruder.

name: Lucy Best: Unbelievable
description:
Most people have an unbelievable, fantastical story from their life. Thanks to an unconventional life, Lucy Best has collected more than most.

In her latest intimate one-woman storytelling show, Lucy unpacks a treasure trove of real-life tales that are often hard to believe.

Expect clowning, impressions and plenty of laughs.

Part of the Melbourne International Comedy Festival.

Celebrating the flavours of their childhoods in the Philippines and Vietnam, this four-course share-style feast transforms street food faves and family recipes into refined modern temptations.

From crispy anchovy quail and poached duck congee to cornfed chicken dumplings and br

name: Art of the Score: James Horner
description:
Art of the Score returns to present the music of the legendary Hollywood composer James Horner.

Known for his iconic scores for Braveheart, Apollo 13, Aliens and many more, the composer also won the Academy Award for Titanic.

Featuring:
- Melbourne Symphony Orchestra
- Nicholas Buc, conductor
- Andrew Pogson, presenter
- Dr Dan Golding, presenter

description:
Charlie Pickering is taking TGIF! to the Melbourne International Comedy Festival.

TGIF! – Thank God It’s Friday! is a weekly comedy radio show airing every Friday, live from the ABC studios. These three Melbourne International Comedy Festival special editions will recap the week’s events with some of the best comedians from the Festival.

Join Charlie and his guests for an hour of laughs – the perfect way to kick start the weekend.

This event is free but get in quick, tickets will go fast!

Designed to complement a modern lifestyle, with its floating salon and private central courtyard, Walsh Street lent itself to sophisticated entertaining. On this tour, your guide will share anecdotes of one of Melbourne’s hot-ticket party houses – as mixologists serve signature 1960s-inspired cocktails. You’ll then be free to discover Walsh Street as the dusk light dances across this unique architectural masterpiece.
 
The Robin Boyd Foundation, in collaboration with Four Pillars Gin and Life's

Developed by Dr. Gunther von Hagens, inventor of Plastination, and curated by physician and designer Dr. Angelina Whalley, this special edition offers a unique exploration of the human body, emotions, and what it means to live a healthy and fulfilling life.

In celebration of the French Film Festival’s 37th anniversary, the Alliance Française de Melbourne and Sofitel Melbourne On Collins have curated a unique experience.

Enjoy an exclusive evening of culinary delight and breathtaking panoramic city views. The Sofitel Melbourne On Collins’ Executive Chef, John Savage has designed a unique 3-course dinner thoughtfully prepared and inspired by the film Redress.

This vibrant festival showcased Indonesia’s rich culture and diverse cuisine, offering locals and visitors a taste of Indonesian traditions through captivating exhibitions and authentic culinary experiences.

The event is aimed to celebrate and promote Indonesian culture, bringing its flavours and heritage to a wider audience.

Rising from the ovens of the state’s most extraordinary bakeries, Baker’s Dozen, presented by McKenzie’s, returns to the Melbourne Food and Wine Festival for a fourth year. And like a sourdough loaf growing high in a Scotch oven, everyone’s favourite celebration of caking and baking is expanding, with way more bakers, plus some fantastic guests from interstate and overseas.

No one knows the 'why' or 'how' of baking better than Helen Goh, recipe developer with Yotam Ottolenghi for more than a decade, co-author of bestselling books  Sweet  and  Comfort, food columnist for the Good Weekend, The Guardian and Observer, and practising psychologist.

A jury of leading comedy and film industry professionals have spent hours chuckling in front of their screens, and now it’s your chance to catch the 2026 finalist films back‑to‑back. Join Swiss-Australian composer and pianist Amy Rita for a Piano Day concert at Opera Australia.

Piano Day is celebrated on the 88th day of the year - a tribute to the 88 keys of the piano. This performance features a selection of original works for solo piano.

Doors open from 6:30pm.

name: Chris Pucillo: A South African Son
description:
Join Chris Pucillo as he dives into the bizarre and chaotic experience of growing up in 1990s South Africa, and unpacks the strange intersections of history, trauma and family.

Blending absurdity with poignant reflections, this show hilariously explores how surviving an upbringing during the birth of a nation shapes a new generation of parenting – and why some scars just have to be laughed off.

Part of the Melbourne International Comedy Festival.

Wander the galleries at your own pace, away from the daytime crowds, and discover new perspectives through a curated program of talks and tours offering rare behind-the-scenes insights into their exhibitions and collections.

Tricky Nick has planned the most amazing magic in the world. But, when it comes to this manic magician, things don’t always go to plan. Strap in for an unpredictable rollercoaster ride through the weird mind of Tricky Nick.

Melbourne is home to some of the most creative artists and designers and CRFT* WRK is your chance to catch them all in the one place.

Browse unique stalls, meet the artists behind the work and indulge in delicious treats from artisan goods to classic Italian favourites, while enjoying the lively atmosphere of Lygon Street.

name: Sonia Di Iorio: Right Here, Right Now
description:
With over 15 years of stand-up at comedy clubs, festivals and events, Sonia has made her mark on stages across the country. 

Right Here, Right Now is loaded with gags about trying to live in the moment, all performed with Sonia's signature wit and fearless storytelling.

name: Head Thorax Abdomen
description:
Wendy, David and Isha are trapped inside a terrible mystery. Addressed by an unseen voice, they struggle to piece together the narratives of their interconnected lives and solve this surreal puzzle before it is too late. Welcome to their confusion and panic.

Part jet-black comedy, part post-modern thriller, part tragedy – this is a must-see for anyone who contemplates life’s un-lived possibilities.

name: Aled Jones: My Story, My Songs
description:
The My Story, My Songs tour from Aled Jones gives fans a rare chance to get up close and personal with one of the country’s most loved performers.

A witty raconteur, Aled also performs the hits from his extraordinary career, and a self-penned track from his Number One album Full Circle.

Be our guest as the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra performs the score to Disney’s animated film classic, Beauty and the Beast, live in concert.

This magical film, overflowing with unforgettable characters and Academy Award-winning music by Alan Menken, has been capturing the hearts and minds of generations old and new with its classic tale of love and enchantment. Experience this spellbinding story backed by the full might of the MSO!

This seasonal experience features an artfully curated selection of handcrafted desserts, Easter-inspired delights and elegant savoury creations, beautifully presented across classic tiered stands.

Infused with great comedy and plenty of audience participation, the magic will feel up-close and personal in this bespoke venue.

The Little Food Festival is a celebration of possibility – two vibrant days of hands-on activities to spark curiosity and excitement about the entire food system.

From the soil where food is grown to the tables where it’s shared, the festival showcases the magic of food and the role we all play in shaping a sustainable future.

The Makers and Shakers Market is back at the iconic Royal Exhibition Building with its biggest lineup yet, with 185+ stalls of Australian-made homewares, food and fashion.

As Victoria’s only makers market partnered with the Australian Made Campaign, every product is designed and made locally, making conscious, sustainable shopping fun and easy.

Enjoy low-cost craft workshops for all ages, free tastings, delicious sips, and plenty of good vibes.

Plus, it’s the ten-year anniversary of The Maker

The action-packed adventure pits man against prehistoric predators in the ultimate battle for survival. Featuring visually stunning imagery and groundbreaking special effects, this epic film is sheer movie magic 65 million years in the making.

Now audiences can experience Jurassic Park projected in HD with the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra performing John Williams’ iconic score live to picture.

Dive into the scenes, sounds and stories that shaped entire generations. Music Documented brings together films that capture music as a cultural force – from subcultures built in bedrooms and back alleys to artists who shifted attitudes and ignited movements. These films tap into the heartbeat of pop culture and the people who lived it. See the moments when music made us feel seen.

name: Charli English: Portholes
description:
Charli English's exhibition presents a series of hybrid painting-sculpture works that examine the intersection of coastal erosion and the housing crisis, two converging forces that are reshaping access to beauty and belonging in contemporary Australia.

The works combine large-scale landscape paintings with three-dimensional fabric curtains, creating immersive installations that blur the boundary between painting and sculpture. Each piece is constructed primarily from reclaimed canvases, salvage

name: Dining at the Palace: Collecting British Royal Tableware
description:
From Worcester to Wedgwood, the finest potters have enhanced the splendour of the royal dining table since the dawn of porcelain manufacture in Britain. Successive sovereigns have proven passionate and important patrons of ceramics, each commissioning and collecting pieces of the highest quality. These tactile royal objects offer a window onto the grand banquets and royal dining habits of the past while continuing to contribute to the magnificence of the monarchy today.

In Dining at the Palace,

name: Grass with Live Score by ZÖJ
description:
Grass with live score by ZÖJ is a rare chance to experience one of cinema’s great silent documentaries as a shared, immersive event. Presented by Castlemaine Documentary Festival (CDoc), the screening restores cinema as a collective ritual, where film, sound and audience meet in the same room. 

Made in 1925, Grass follows the seasonal migration of the Bakhtiari people across what is now Iran, a vast journey undertaken by tens of thousands of people and animals. It is a powerful record of endura

name: AFL Round 8: Essendon v Brisbane Lions
description:
Come along to Marvel Stadium on Saturday afternoon to witness the Round 8 clash between Essendon and the Brisbane Lions. 

Get your tickets today!

name: Melbourne Chamber Orchestra: Overgrown Paths
description:
Melbourne Chamber Orchestra's Artistic Director and violinist Sophie Rowell leads a luminous fusion of music and poetry that culminates in The Four Seasons – voiced with Vivaldi’s original sonnets by acclaimed actress and narrator, Helen Morse.

The concert opens with the ebullience of Hugo Wolf’s Italian Serenade, followed by Libby Croad’s Portraits for string orchestra, each movement paired with evocative poetry by Eleanor Percy.

Janáček’s tender On an Overgrown Path invites a quiet turn inwa

name: Mother's Day Lunch Cruise
description:
This Mother’s Day, spoil your mum with a relaxing three-hour lunch cruise along the scenic Yarra River because she deserves to sit back, relax, and be thoroughly spoiled.

Discover the beautiful city of Melbourne from a unique perspective as you cruise past iconic attractions including the Royal Botanic Gardens, the Melbourne Cricket Ground and sporting precinct, Federation Square, and Southbank.

Enjoy a delicious three-course lunch with all-inclusive beverages, so there’s no need to top up a g

name: Hyperworlds: Unreal Cartography
description:
An exhibition from Allanah Sarafian exploring maps as emotional and ideological objects.

Inspired by medieval mapping methods where the church occupied the ideological centre and the margins teemed with the strange and unknown; this collection turns toward the periphery. Sculptural textile terrains focus on the 'othered' edges of belief systems – the imagined, the misremembered and the speculative. Living within a diaspora, gaps in knowledge are often filled with invention. These works chart th

name: AFL Round 10: Melbourne v Hawthorn
description: Come along to the 'G to get involved in this year’s Sir Doug Nicholls Round. Watch Melbourne take on Hawthorn on Saturday afternoon live.

name: Mass of Deliverance
description:
The human voice is an instrument that connects us all. Hear it sing through two radiant pieces of music from England’s beloved choral composer John Rutter. His 1970s Gloria begins with a surprisingly high-energy fanfare, and his Hymn projects sacred traditions into our modern era. MSO Chorus and musicians will perform these elevating works in the Melbourne Recital Centre, led by conductor Warren Trevelyan-Jones.

Dan Walker’s Mass of Deliverance brings a local voice to our contemporary choral pr

name: Echoes of Piazzolla: Zero Hour
description:
Featuring repertoire from the eponymous album Tango: Zero Hour, the concert traces Astor Piazzolla’s journey to becoming one of the most renowned musicians and composers in history.

Experience Piazzolla’s music in his trademark ensemble that revolutionised tango and classical music – the quintet. A perfect balance of instrumentation accompanied by authentic artistic interpretation, witness the five-piece Australian-Argentinian-Uruguayan ensemble Canberra Tango Quintet perform on the very instru

name: RISING: Melbourne
description:
RISING, Melbourne’s premiere winter festival of new art, music and performance, returns to showcase the city in all its moon-lit glory.

Over 12 nights, RISING turns up the volume on the music and movement that shapes Melbourne. Featuring Lil' Kim, Kae Tempest, Daniel Avery, Florentina Holzinger and plenty more.

Log off and let loose.

name: AFL Round 12: Western Bulldogs v Collingwood
description: Watch the Bulldogs and the Pies play live at Marvel Stadium on Saturday night – it's a game you won't want to miss.

name: Korean Drama and Cinema Reimagined in Jazz
description:
From Crash Landing on You to Parasite, Korean drama and film music has captured hearts worldwide. For one special night at the Paris Cat, pianist-composer Mina Yu brings these unforgettable melodies into the world of jazz with her quartet – tender, soulful and alive with improvisation.

With an engaging blend of storytelling and music, Mina combines musical performances with cultural insights and spoken Korean/English, creating an immersive and atmospheric experience. 

A night for friends and l

name: Winter Market
description:
Whether you’re looking for great food, unique shopping or just a fun night out, the Winter Wonderland Night Market offers something for everyone.

This festive night market is organised and run by William Angliss Institute event management students, giving students the opportunity to showcase their skills and gain real-world event experience.

This event is also being held as a charity fundraiser in partnership with Foodbank Victoria, helping support people in the community experiencing food ins

name: RISING: Saul Williams Meets Carlos Niño & Friends
description:
For over 50 years the conservationists at TreePeople have been helping protect the hills up in Coldwater Canyon National Park, just off Mulholland Drive. It’s here, under black walnut trees and oaks, that Saul Williams met up with Carlos Niño and six other members of LA jazz’s new guard to record an open-hearted missive of revolutionary thought and melodic release.

The set was a return of sorts for Williams, who helped popularise slam poetry in the late 90s with the film SLAM (which he wrote an

name: The Linda Ronstadt Show
description:
Experience a dedicated Linda Ronstadt show, with Lisa Mio and Wild Dreamers.

Allow Lisa Mio, known for her work with Brian Cadd's Bootleg Family Band, Vanessa Amorosi, Rogue Traders, and more, lead you with enchanting vocals, accompanied by an all‑star ensemble featuring Jo George, Gary Young, Nigel Davis, Cres Crisp, and Phil Smith.

Mio not only captivates with masterful renditions from Ronstadt’s extensive songbook, but she also weaves lyrical dialogue that offers a glimpse into Ronstadt’s j

name: AFL Round 13: Essendon v Carlton
description: Celebrate the long weekend on King’s Birthday Eve by watching Essendon play Carlton live at the 'G.

name: Dining with Jane Austen with Lise Rodgers
description:
'We are to have company on Friday!....we are to be nine at table! I am most desirous of providing not just a bill of fare suitable to such distinguished company, but one that will cause no embarrassment to our larder – or more particularly, to she who is its keeper.'

It is every woman’s dilemma. Guests for dinner – what to serve? For women in Jane Austen’s time it was no different. Searching for recipes and new ideas, gathering ingredients, arranging the table, preparing the food, then serving

name: Melbourne Magic Festival
description:
The Melbourne Magic Festival casts its spell across Melbourne, promising to take you to the extreme end of visual theatre with over 140 performances of 40 different shows, including not one but three top international guest magicians from all over Australia.

Be amazed as Melbourne becomes a magical hub in winter for 14 days of special events, workshops and live shows for everyone from child-friendly to adults-only.

Bring the family for a big day out of magic shows and workshops and grab lunch

name: Melbourne Tartan Festival
description:
Beginning at the Spring Street end of Collins Street at 2pm, approximately 200 pipers, drummers, dancers, clan and society representatives, plus some gorgeous wee Scottie dogs, will parade down Collins Street in celebration of all things Scottish.

There'll also be piping and Highland dancing displays between 11.30am and 1.30pm at Old Treasury Building terrace and pipers on the balconies from 1pm. Head to the Robbie Burns Statue in Treasury Gardens for Burns’ poetry readings.

Don’t miss the pop

name: Vera Blue x MSO
description:
Vera Blue and the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra unite for a one-night collision of intimacy and scale, where electronic pulse meets full symphonic force, and song expands into something cinematic and elemental.

Reimagined in orchestral form, Vera Blue’s catalogue opens into new dimension, shaped and elevated by the sweep of strings, brass and woodwinds. The orchestra becomes a living, breathing veil around the songs, deepening quiet moments into widescreen emotion and drawing each melody into so

name: Heartbreak Hotel
description:
After a sell-out season at last year's RISING festival, Wellington-based company EBKM brings back their cathartic comedy that explores what heartbreak does to our bodies and minds.

Dressed in lavender tassels and backed by covers of breakup songs (Elvis, Celine, the real heavy hitters), Karin McCracken takes us on a tour through the aching limbo of a serious break up. She evokes famous novels and scientific research. She confronts her exes, all of whom are played by the virtuosic Simon Leary. S

name: Mackenzie
description:
What if MacBeth was a 13-year-old child star? A camp, dark and deranged retelling of Macbeth.

Mackenzie is 13 and she’s booked her first-ever TV gig: a tiny part on a huge kids show. It’s a small start, but Mackenzie’s mum has always felt sure that her daughter was destined for superstardom. One day, Mackenzie and her best on-set friend Beau encounter a geriatric make-up artist who is struck with a vision – that Mackenzie will be the number one pop girl of the world.

Set in the heights of earl

name: Flinders Quartet: New Light
description:
The final concert of the year for the Flinders Quartet shines a 'new light' on the quartet tradition.

Beethoven’s String Quartet in E-flat major, Op.127 – the first of his groundbreaking late quartets –  opens the program with majestic chords and profound emotional range, followed by Grażyna Bacewicz's Quartet No.3. Completing the program is a world premiere by Naomi Dodd, an Australian composer whose music demonstrates keen intelligence and natural affinity for the string quartet form.

name: Geelong Running Festival 2026
description: Annual Geelong Running Festival featuring multiple distance events for runners of all levels.

name: Brahms Festival: Second Symphony and Piano Concerto
description:
Brahms' Symphony No 2 carries the pastoral feel of the Austrian summer in which he wrote the music, and while he belittled its success to his friends, you will hear nothing but a remarkable composition.

Pianist Denis Kozhukhin continues his role as soloist for Brahms’ Piano Concerto No 2, presenting two Brahms symphonies played back to back.

name: Brahms Festival: Third and Fourth Symphonies
description:
If you love Brahms, you’ll hear his large-scale compositions brought to life with a world-class orchestra. And if you’re completely new to the music, you’ll quickly discover what one of the most important composers brought to the world. Brahms’ third and fourth symphonies project honesty and emotional expression from an artist who never quite escaped his perfectionism, regardless of how successful he grew. He was humble and self-deprecating, and had a weak spot for his friends and loved ones.

name: The Trumpet of the Swan
description:
Louis is a young swan born without a voice. With the help of a boy named Sam and a stolen trumpet, he sets out to prove that even the quietest among us can make the loudest impact.

A novel symphony for actors and orchestra, The Trumpet of the Swan brings E.B. White’s classic novel to the stage with lively narration performed by actors and a cinematic, lyrical score played live by Orchestra Victoria.

This charming testament to the power of music wowed critics at its premiere. It’s perfect for p

name: Home Alone in Concert
description:
Experience the beloved comedy classic featuring renowned composer John Williams’ charming and delightful score performed live to picture by the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra.

Written and produced by John Hughes and directed by Chris Columbus, Home Alone (1990) is a hilarious and heart-warming film starring Macaulay Culkin as Kevin McCallister, an 8-year-old boy who’s accidentally left behind when his family leaves for Christmas vacation, and who must defend his home against two bungling thieves.
